opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: It feels like an elephant is sitting on the center of my chest, and my jaw is aching terribly.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Sudden severe chest pain and jaw ache that started less than an hour ago and isn't going away.

symptom_narrative.onset: sudden, about 45 minutes ago, while sitting at rest
symptom_narrative.duration: constant since it started, not improving
symptom_narrative.character: crushing, like an elephant sitting on my chest
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: severe, the worst pain I've ever had in my chest
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: right in the center of my chest, going into my left arm and my jaw
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant, hasn't changed with sitting or moving
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing, changing position hasn't helped at all
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: nothing specific, it just started while I was resting
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: sweating a lot, feel short of breath, felt sick once, and I have this awful feeling like something terrible is about to happen
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: never had anything like this before, no history of angina
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: can't do anything, just sitting gripping my chest

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: spouse at home, live a bit further out of town so ambulance may take longer to arrive

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: terrified, wants immediate reassurance and help

communication_style.typical_message_style: short, urgent messages, spouse may help communicate

access_barriers.transport: rural/outer-regional location — longer ambulance response time anticipated
